exploits/windows/wins/ms04_045_wins.name: MS04-045 Microsoft WINS Service Memory Overwrite
exploits/windows/wins/ms04_045_wins.description:
This module exploits an arbitrary memory write flaw in the
          WINS service. This exploit has been tested against Windows
          2000 only.
exploits/windows/wins/ms04_045_wins.reason: Targets built-in Windows WINS service for remote code execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/winrm/winrm_script_exec.name: WinRM Script Exec Remote Code Execution
exploits/windows/winrm/winrm_script_exec.description:
This module uses valid credentials to login to the WinRM service
          and execute a payload. It has two available methods for payload
          delivery: Powershell 2 (and above) and VBS CmdStager.

          The module will check if Powershell is available, and if so uses
          that method. Otherwise it falls back to the VBS CmdStager which is
          less stealthy.
exploits/windows/winrm/winrm_script_exec.reason: Targets Windows WinRM service and achieves remote code execution with valid credentials.

exploits/windows/vpn/safenet_ike_11.name: SafeNet SoftRemote IKE Service Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/vpn/safenet_ike_11.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in Safenet SoftRemote IKE IreIKE.exe
          service. When sending a specially crafted udp packet to port 62514 an
          attacker may be able to execute arbitrary code. This module has
          been tested with Juniper NetScreen-Remote 10.8.0 (Build 20) using
          windows/meterpreter/reverse_ord_tcp payloads.
exploits/windows/vpn/safenet_ike_11.reason: Targets Windows VPN software with remote code execution via UDP packet without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/vnc/realvnc_client.name: RealVNC 3.3.7 Client Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/vnc/realvnc_client.description: This module exploits a buffer overflow in RealVNC 3.3.7 (vncviewer.exe).
exploits/windows/vnc/realvnc_client.reason: Requires victim to connect with RealVNC client, making it client-side.

exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_client.name: UltraVNC 1.0.1 Client Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_client.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in UltraVNC Win32
          Viewer 1.0.1 Release.
exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_client.reason: Requires victim to connect to attacker server (client-side attack)

exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_viewer_bof.name: UltraVNC 1.0.2 Client (vncviewer.exe) Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_viewer_bof.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in UltraVNC Viewer 1.0.2 Release.

          If a malicious server responds to a client connection indicating a minor
          protocol version of 14 or 16, a 32-bit integer is subsequently read from
          the TCP stream by the client and directly provided as the trusted size for
          further reading from the TCP stream into a 1024-byte character array on
          the stack.
exploits/windows/vnc/ultravnc_viewer_bof.reason: Requires victim to actively connect to malicious server with UltraVNC viewer client.

exploits/windows/vnc/winvnc_http_get.name: WinVNC Web Server GET Overflow
exploits/windows/vnc/winvnc_http_get.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in the AT&T WinVNC version
          <= v3.3.3r7 web server. When debugging mode with logging is
          enabled (non-default), an overly long GET request can overwrite
          the stack. This exploit does not work well with VNC payloads!
exploits/windows/vnc/winvnc_http_get.reason: Targets Windows NT4 with AT&T WinVNC web server, requires only network access and non-default debug logging enabled.

exploits/windows/unicenter/cam_log_secur.name: CA CAM log_security() Stack Buffer Overflow (Win32)
exploits/windows/unicenter/cam_log_secur.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability in the CA CAM service
          by passing a long parameter to the log_security() function.
          The CAM service is part of TNG Unicenter. This module has
          been tested on Unicenter v3.1.
exploits/windows/unicenter/cam_log_secur.reason: Targets Windows CA CAM service with remote buffer overflow, no victim interaction required.

exploits/windows/tftp/attftp_long_filena.name: Allied Telesyn TFTP Server 1.9 Long Filename Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/attftp_long_filena.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in AT-TFTP v1.9, by sending a
          request (get/write) for an overly long file name.
exploits/windows/tftp/attftp_long_filena.reason: Remote buffer overflow in Windows TFTP service requires only network access.

exploits/windows/tftp/distinct_tftp_trav.name: Distinct TFTP 3.10 Writable Directory Traversal Execution
exploits/windows/tftp/distinct_tftp_trav.description:
This module exploits a directory traversal vulnerability in the TFTP
          Server component of Distinct Intranet Servers version 3.10 which
          allows a remote attacker to write arbitrary files to the server file
          system, resulting in code execution under the context of 'SYSTEM'.
          This module has been tested successfully on TFTP Server version 3.10
          on Windows XP SP3 (EN).
exploits/windows/tftp/distinct_tftp_trav.reason: Targets Windows TFTP server software allowing remote file write leading to code execution without user interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/dlink_long_filenam.name: D-Link TFTP 1.0 Long Filename Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/dlink_long_filenam.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in D-Link TFTP 1.0.
          By sending a request for an overly long file name, an attacker
          could overflow a buffer and execute arbitrary code. For best results,
          use bind payloads with nonx (No NX).
exploits/windows/tftp/dlink_long_filenam.reason: Targets Windows 2000 SP4 via network TFTP service with no user interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/futuresoft_transfe.name: FutureSoft TFTP Server 2000 Transfer-Mode Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/futuresoft_transfe.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the FutureSoft TFTP Server
          2000 product. By sending an overly long transfer-mode string, we were able
          to overwrite both the SEH and the saved EIP. A subsequent write-exception
          that will occur allows the transferring of execution to our shellcode
          via the overwritten SEH. This module has been tested against Windows
          2000 Professional and for some reason does not seem to work against
          Windows 2000 Server (could not trigger the overflow at all).
exploits/windows/tftp/futuresoft_transfe.reason: Targets Windows 2000 Pro via network TFTP service with no victim interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/netdecision_tftp_t.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in NetDecision 4.2 TFTP server. The
          software contains a directory traversal vulnerability that allows a remote attacker
          to write arbitrary file to the file system, which results in code  execution under
          the context of user executing the TFTP Server.
targets: NetDecision 4.2 TFTP on Windows XP SP3 / Windows 2003 SP2
exploits/windows/tftp/netdecision_tftp_t.reason: Exploits a network service vulnerability without user interaction and targets Windows.

exploits/windows/tftp/opentftp_error_cod.name: OpenTFTP SP 1.4 Error Packet Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/opentftp_error_cod.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in OpenTFTP Server SP 1.4.  The vulnerable
          condition triggers when the TFTP opcode is configured as an error packet, the TFTP
          service will then format the message using a sprintf() function, which causes an
          overflow, therefore allowing remote code execution under the context of SYSTEM.

          The offset (to EIP) is specific to how the TFTP was started (as a 'Stand Alone',
          or 'Service').  By default the target is set to 'Service' because that's the default
          configuration during OpenTFTP Server SP 1.4's installation.
exploits/windows/tftp/opentftp_error_cod.reason: Targets Windows with remote code execution via network TFTP service without user interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/quick_tftp_pro_mod.name: Quick FTP Pro 2.1 Transfer-Mode Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/quick_tftp_pro_mod.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in the Quick TFTP Pro server
          product. MS Update KB926436 screws up the opcode address being used in oledlg.dll resulting
          in a DoS.  This is a port of a sploit by Mati "muts" Aharoni.
exploits/windows/tftp/quick_tftp_pro_mod.reason: Targets Windows and achieves remote code execution via network without victim interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/tftpd32_long_filen.name: TFTPD32 Long Filename Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpd32_long_filen.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in TFTPD32 version 2.21
          and prior. By sending a request for an overly long file name
          to the tftpd32 server, a remote attacker could overflow a buffer and
          execute arbitrary code on the system.
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpd32_long_filen.reason: Remote buffer overflow in TFTPD32 service requires only network access and no victim interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/tftpdwin_long_file.name: TFTPDWIN v0.4.2 Long Filename Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpdwin_long_file.description:
This module exploits the ProSysInfo TFTPDWIN threaded TFTP Server. By sending
          an overly long file name to the tftpd.exe server, the stack can be overwritten.
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpdwin_long_file.reason: Exploits a network service (TFTP) without requiring victim interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/tftpserver_wrq_bof.name: TFTP Server for Windows 1.4 ST WRQ Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpserver_wrq_bof.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in TFTP Server 1.4 ST.  The flaw
          is due to the way TFTP handles the filename parameter extracted from a WRQ request.
          The server will append the user-supplied filename to TFTP server binary's path
          without any bounds checking, and then attempt to check this path with a fopen().
          Since this isn't a valid file path, fopen() returns null, which allows the
          corrupted data to be used in a strcmp() function, causing an access violation.

          Since the offset is sensitive to how the TFTP server is launched, you must know
          in advance if your victim machine launched the TFTP as a 'Service' or 'Standalone'
          , and then manually select your target accordingly. A successful attempt will lead
          to remote code execution under the context of SYSTEM if run as a service, or
          the user if run as a standalone. A failed attempt will result a denial-of-service.
targets: Windows XP SP2/SP3 EN Service Mode
exploits/windows/tftp/tftpserver_wrq_bof.reason: Targets Windows TFTP service with remote code execution via network without user interaction.

exploits/windows/tftp/threectftpsvc_long.name: 3CTftpSvc TFTP Long Mode Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/tftp/threectftpsvc_long.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in 3CTftpSvc 2.0.1. By
          sending a specially crafted packet with an overly long mode
          field, a remote attacker could overflow a buffer and execute
          arbitrary code on the system.
exploits/windows/tftp/threectftpsvc_long.reason: TFTP server is third-party software that can be installed and run on Windows for testing.

exploits/windows/telnet/gamsoft_telsrv_u.name: GAMSoft TelSrv 1.5 Username Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/telnet/gamsoft_telsrv_u.description:
This module exploits a username sprintf stack buffer overflow in GAMSoft TelSrv 1.5.
          Other versions may also be affected. The service terminates after exploitation,
          so you only get one chance!
exploits/windows/telnet/gamsoft_telsrv_u.reason: Third-party telnet server with remote buffer overflow that can be installed in a VM.

exploits/windows/telnet/goodtech_telnet.name: GoodTech Telnet Server Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/telnet/goodtech_telnet.description:
This module exploits a stack buffer overflow in GoodTech Systems Telnet Server
          versions prior to 5.0.7. By sending an overly long string, an attacker can
          overwrite the buffer and control program execution.
exploits/windows/telnet/goodtech_telnet.reason: Third-party telnet server software can be installed on Windows VM for remote exploitation.

exploits/windows/ssl/ms04_011_pct.name: MS04-011 Microsoft Private Communications Transport Overflow
exploits/windows/ssl/ms04_011_pct.description:
This module exploits a buffer overflow in the Microsoft
          Windows SSL PCT protocol stack. This code is based on Johnny
          Cyberpunk's THC release and has been tested against Windows
          2000 and Windows XP. To use this module, specify the remote
          port of any SSL service, or the port and protocol of an
          application that uses SSL. The only application protocol
          supported at this time is SMTP. You only have one chance to
          select the correct target, if you are attacking IIS, you may
          want to try one of the other exploits first (WebDAV). If
          WebDAV does not work, this more than likely means that this
          is either Windows 2000 SP4+ or Windows XP (IIS 5.0 vs IIS
          5.1). Using the wrong target may not result in an immediate
          crash of the remote system.
exploits/windows/ssl/ms04_011_pct.reason: Exploits built-in Windows SSL PCT protocol stack without user interaction.

exploits/windows/ssh/freeftpd_key_exchan.name: FreeFTPd 1.0.10 Key Exchange Algorithm String Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/ssh/freeftpd_key_exchan.description:
This module exploits a simple stack buffer overflow in FreeFTPd 1.0.10
          This flaw is due to a buffer overflow error when handling a specially
          crafted key exchange algorithm string received from an SSH client.
          This module is based on MC's freesshd_key_exchange exploit.
exploits/windows/ssh/freeftpd_key_exchan.reason: Third-party SSH server software can be installed on a Windows VM and exploited remotely without user interaction.

exploits/windows/ssh/freesshd_authbypass.description:
This module exploits a vulnerability found in FreeSSHd <= 1.2.6 to bypass
          authentication. You just need the username (which defaults to root). The exploit
          has been tested with both password and public key authentication.
exploits/windows/ssh/freesshd_authbypass.reason: Targets Windows FreeSSHd service for authentication bypass without user interaction.

exploits/windows/ssh/freesshd_key_exchan.name: FreeSSHd 1.0.9 Key Exchange Algorithm String Buffer Overflow
exploits/windows/ssh/freesshd_key_exchan.description:
This module exploits a simple stack buffer overflow in FreeSSHd 1.0.9.
          This flaw is due to a buffer overflow error when handling a specially
          crafted key exchange algorithm string received from an SSH client.
exploits/windows/ssh/freesshd_key_exchan.reason: Targets Windows with network-based SSH service and no victim interaction required.
